title: Gooey Floral Malaysian Tofu Curry
description: A one-pot, vegan-friendly Malaysian dish featuring the sweetness of balsamic vinegar and the warmth of tandoori spices, all coming together in a gooey, floral curry.
instructions: Heat oil in a large, deep skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the grated ginger and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cubed tofu and cook until it starts to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the tandoori masala and stir to coat the tofu evenly. Cook for 1 minute.
Pour in the coconut milk, balsamic vinegar, and salt. Stir well to combine.
Reduce heat to low and let simmer for 20 minutes, or until the sauce thickens.
Mix the jasmine rice flour with a little water to make a slurry, then add it to the curry to thicken.
If using rose petal jam, stir it in during the last 5 minutes of cooking.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs or edible flowers if desired.
